title: Committed review applies singleton no-list exception-aware review

provenance.originSummary: Derived from a sanitized real PR or review incident.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark the exception-aware singleton no-list review behavior added after the Durable Task retention-policy false positive.
provenance.genericCondition: The singleton resource is modeled as a fixed child endpoint under its parent. The historical failure mode is that review notices an SDK list method and raises a generic missing-list-resource issue instead of applying the exception-aware singleton path.

description: A sanitized committed-review scenario new-resource PR where one resource is a singleton child configuration object. The SDK package exposes a list method, but the Terraform resource models a fixed child endpoint per parent, so the review should not raise a plain missing-list-resource issue.

scope.notes: The singleton resource is modeled as a fixed child endpoint under its parent. The modeled failure mode is that review notices an SDK list method and raises a generic missing-list-resource issue instead of applying the exception-aware singleton path.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should recognize singleton-child implementation evidence and avoid a plain missing-list-resource issue for that resource.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not raise a generic missing-list-resource issue for the singleton child resource just because the SDK package exposes a list method.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the exception-aware singleton no-list review behavior added after the Durable Task retention-policy false positive.
